person.name: Geoffrey De Saye II
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Geoffrey de Saye, Knt., 2nd Lord Say, son and heir of Geoffrey de Say, Knt. and Idoine de Leybourne, was born 30 April 1305.

He had lands in West Greenwich, Birling, Burham, Cudham, Deptford and Patrixbourne, Kent; Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire; Buxted and Hamsey, Sussex, etc. He was Admiral of the Fleet from the mouth of the Thames westward; Keeper of the Chaces of Surrey, Sussex, Middlesex and Bucks; and Constable of Rochester Castle from 1354-1359

He was seventeen when his father died in 1322. John Triple, citizen of London, bought Geoffrey's wardship and marriage from the king on 11 January 1323. In June 1326 he proved his age and received seisin of his father's lands. In 1333 he obtained rights in his mother's former dower lands.

He was a soldier, serving in the Scottish expedition in 1327. He was summoned to parliament in 1333 and attended the Dunstable tournament. He served overseas in the king's service in 1330/1, 1331/2, 1332/3 and also at Berwick in 1334 and Newcastle in 1335. He served at Flanders in 1338 and was at Roxborough in January 1356 to witness the Anglo-Scottish treaty.

He married Maud de Beauchamp, daughter of Guy de Beauchamp, Knt., 10th Earl of Warwick, and his second wife, Alice de Tony. The date and place of their marriage is unknown. 

«b»They had three sons and three daughters:«/b»
1.) William, Knt., 3rd Lord Say, born 17 Jun 1340, died before 7 August 1375, married Beatrice de Brewes and had two children

2.) Thomas, Knt., died without issue

3.) John, Knt., died testate before 29 September 1410 without issue

4.) Idoine, wife of Sir John de Clinton, 3rd Lord Clinton

5.) Elizabeth, died before 15 May 1401, wife of Sir Thomas de Aldon

6.) Joan, died 29 June 1378, wife of Sir William de Fiennes and of Sir Stephen de Valoines

Sir Geoffrey de Saye, 2nd Baron Say, died testate on 26 June 1359. The wardship and marriage of his son and heir, 19 year-old William, was given to Queen Phillipa. His widow, Maud, remarried to Edmund ______ and she died testate 28 July 1369, her will requesting burial at the church of the Black Friars, London near "Edmund, my beloved husband".

«b»Research Notes
Manor of Saye's Court«/b»
"William his son succeeded him, and died anno 56 Henry III. holding this manor in capite by barony, and the repair of a house in Dover-castle, called, from its possessors, Saye's-tower. His son of the same name, accounted at the Exchequer for twenty-seven fees of the honour of Magminot; that is, twenty-six of the old feoffment and one of the new, which shews the large extent of his possessions. He died anno 23 king Edward I. possessed of this manor, leaving Geoffrey his son and heir, who married Idonea, daughter of William de Leyborne (who survived him) and died possessed of it, in the 15th of king Edward II. leaving Geoffrey de Saye, his son, who, in the 8th of king Edward III. obtained the king's charter for free warren for all his demean lands in his lordships of Greenwich, Deptford, &c. with the view of frankpledge and other privileges, and died in the 33d year of it, leaving William his son and heir, and Maud his wife, surviving, (daughter of Guy de Beauchamp, earl of Warwick) and three daughters, who afterwards became their brother's heirs, as will be further mentioned."

«b»Sawbridgeworth Manor«/b»
"Geoffrey de Saye, his son, was summoned to Parliament as Lord Say from 1313. In 1306 he obtained a renewal of the Friday market granted in 1223 and a grant of a yearly fair on the vigil and feast of the Nativity of the Virgin Mary (8 September). He died in 1322; his wife Idonia survived him and received a grant of the manor for life from her son Geoffrey. Geoffrey the younger died in 1359 and was succeeded by his son William de Say. The extent of the manor taken at his death in 1375 gives a messuage with garden, 500 acres of arable land, 15 acres of meadow, 20 acres of pasture and 100 acres of wood. The rents from customary tenants included 1 lb. of wax and 3 gross of arrows. John son and heir of William died a minor in 1382. The manor passed to his sister Elizabeth, who made a settlement on herself and her first husband, Sir John de Falwesle, in 1388, and on herself and second husband, Sir William Heron, in 1396."

sourceList.title: Calendar of Inquisitions Post Mortem: Volume 10, Edward III

zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey De Saye II於1305年4月30日出生在英國時，他的父親Geoffrey de Saye24歲，母親Idoine Leybourne25歲。他於大約1334年與Mary de Langley在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1359年6月26日在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年54歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey De Saye II于1305年4月30日出生在英国时，他的父亲Geoffrey de Saye24岁，母亲Idoine Leybourne25岁。他于大约1334年与Mary de Langley在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1359年6月26日在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年54岁，埋葬在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey De Saye II was born on 30 April 1305, in England, his father, Geoffrey de Saye, was 24 and his mother, Idoine Leybourne, was 25. He married Mary de Langley about 1334, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 26 June 1359, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 54, and was buried in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey De Saye II est né le 30 avril 1305, à Angleterre, son père, Geoffrey de Saye, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Idoine Leybourne, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Mary de Langley environ 1334, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 26 juin 1359, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey De Saye II am 30. April 1305 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey de Saye 24 und seine Mutter Idoine Leybourne 25. Er hat um 1334 in England Mary de Langley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 26. Juni 1359 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England und wurde in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey De Saye II è nato il il 30 aprile 1305 nel England, il suo padre, Geoffrey de Saye, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Idoine Leybourne, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Mary de Langley circa 1334, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 26 giugno 1359, nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey De Saye IIが1305年4月30日にイギリスで生まれた時，父Geoffrey de Sayeは24歳，母Idoine Leybourneは25歳であった。約1334年にイギリスでMary de Langleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1359年6月26日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスで54歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey De Saye II(이)가 1305년 4월 30일 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey de Saye(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Idoine Leybourne(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1334년 영국에서 Mary de Langley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1359년 6월 26일 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에 묻혔다.

pt.30 April 1305–26 June 1359: 30 de abril de 1305–26 de junho de 1359
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey De Saye II nasceu em 30 de abril de 1305, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Geoffrey de Saye, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Idoine Leybourne, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary de Langley aproximadamente 1334, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 26 de junho de 1359, em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey De Saye II родился 30 апреля 1305 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Geoffrey de Saye было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Idoine Leybourne, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary de Langley около 1334 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 26 июня 1359 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.30 April 1305–26 June 1359: 30 de abril de 1305–26 de junio de 1359
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey De Saye II nació el 30 de abril de 1305, en Inglaterra, su papá, Geoffrey de Saye, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Idoine Leybourne, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Mary de Langley aproximadamente 1334, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 26 de junio de 1359, en Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
